title: Varicose vein symptoms
source_review_basis: full authoritative documents inspected and archived; item-level retain/rewrite/remove/add comparisons recorded

assessment.text: Supports clinician documentation of pain, aching, discomfort, swelling, heaviness, itching, skin change, bleeding, thrombophlebitis, ulcer and patient questions without asserting complication.
assessment.rationale: Relevant to entered symptoms and complication discussion without diagnosing varicose veins.

source.title: Varicose veins: diagnosis and management — Recommendations
source.jurisdiction: England and Wales; international evidence requiring UAE adaptation
source.population: Adults with varicose veins, lower-limb symptoms, skin changes, venous ulceration, or venous insufficiency context.
source.setting: Primary care and vascular-service recognition and referral assessment.
source.exclusions: Exact for lower-limb pain, aching, discomfort, swelling, heaviness, itching, pigmentation, eczema, and chronic-venous-insufficiency context. It does not diagnose stasis dermatitis or venous eczema and does not generate referral or treatment. This batch additionally reviews adult symptom, complication, referral, duplex and pregnancy recommendations while preserving the previously registered chronic-venous-skin and ulcer-history sections. No diagnosis, imaging or treatment is generated.
